titleStmt.title: Ham, Dabra Libānos, Golden Gospel
Golden Gospel of Beta Mata za-Ham

editionStmt.p: Photo Credit for Set photographed in 1993 : Roger Schneider, 1975, scanned by Marie-Laure Derat. © Roger Schneider archive, Institute of Ethiopian Studies, Walda Masqal centre.
Photo Credit for Set photographed in 1993 and Set photographed in 1994: Alessandro Bausi © MIE (Missione Italiana in Eritrea 1993-1994), Consiglio Nazionale delle Ricerche and University of Bologna.

The identification of texts is carried out on the basis of the list in Su alcuni manoscritti presso comunità monastiche dell’Eritrea: Parte terzaRassegna di Studi EtiopiciAlessandroBausi19974113–56, i–ivhttp://www.jstor.org/stable/41299838, and improved with line references aligned to the image segmentation carried out in Transkribus. Units identified where checked against L’evangelo d’oro di Dabra LibānosRendiconti della Reale Accademia dei Lincei, Classe di scienze morali, storiche e filologicheCarloConti Rossini1901Serie quinta, 10177-219http://periodici.librari.beniculturali.it/visualizzatore.aspx?anno=1901&ID_testata=30&ID_periodico=11855 edited text.

The encoding was carried out by Nafisa Valieva thanks to the project HornEast that has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (Grant agreement No. 726206).

desc.title: Gabra Masqal's grant of Sar’e etc

q.ab: ዘወሐቡ፡ ንጉሥ፡ ገብረ፡ መስቀል፡ ለአባ፡ መጣዕ፡ ሰርኤ፡ ለጎ፡ ኦና፡ ሰያባ፡ ወንከብ፡ ስዓጋ፡ ማርያላ፡ ገማዕ፡ ዜና፡ ኵዶ፡ አዲ፡ ሕልሙ፡ ጽዳዕ፡ አሲራ፡ ለባሲሮ፡ ገዳሙሙ፡ ለመነኮሳት፡ ቤተ፡ ሰማዕት፡ አናንያ፡ ወአዛርያ፡ ወሚሳኤል፡ ፭ቱ፡ አዲ፡ በጊኤን፡ ፃዕዳ፡ ብአቲ፡ አዲ፡ አይኬማ፡ አዲ፡ አይሸሑት፡ ምድፉዕ፡ ዋልታ፡ አኦመ፡ ረጌንያ፡ ሰማዝ፡ ጉናጉና፡ ቀዳማዊ፡ ጉልቱ፡ ለአባ፡ መጣዕ፡ በሕይወቱ፡ በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ አነ፡ ገብረ፡ መስቀል፡ ዘንተ፡ ጉልተ፡ ዘወሐብኩ፡ አዕሚርየ፡ ከመ፡ ይሐልፍ፡ ኵ ሉ፡ ብዕል፡ ኵlb ዳወይባ፡ አወገር፡ ፭ቱ፡ አዲ፡ ምስለ፡ ምድሮሙ፡ ሐፉሁ፡ ወመስኖሁ፡ ደረቃ፡ በሲሬ፡

msItem.title: Restoration of gʷǝlt by ‘Amda Ṣǝyon/Garba Masqal
msItem.incipit:
በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡
አጽሐ ፍኩ፡ አነ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ንጉሥ። በ፭፻፲ወ፪ዐመት፡
msItem.explicit:
ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን፡ በዝዓለም፡ ወበዘይመጽእ፡ ዓለም፡ ዘኢይትፈታሕ፡ በጾም፡ ወበጸ
ሎት፡ በስኢል፡ ወበስጊድ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም።
msItem.incipit: ወለንጉሥነ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ መንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ ህየንተ፡ ኅዳጥ፡ ብዙኅ፡ አሜን።
msItem.explicit: የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ሞገስ፡ ከመ፡ ዳዊት፡ ኣሜን። የሀቦ፡ እግዚብሔር፡ ትዕግሥተ፡ ከመ፡ ኢዮብ፡ ኣሜን። የሀቦ፡ እግዚኣብሔር፡ መዊአ፡ ከመ፡ ኢያሱ፡ ኣሜን።

msItem.title: Gʷǝlt by Bǝlen Sābā
msItem.incipit:
በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ በ፭፻፡ ወ፯፡ ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡ ወሀብኩ፡ ኣነ፡
ብሌን፡ ሰባ፡ በዐልተ፡ ቢ
ሐት፡ ብእሲቱ:ለዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ንጉሥ፡
msItem.explicit: የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ትዕግሥተ፡ ከመ፡ እዮብ፡ አሜን። የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ መዊአ፡ ከመ፡ ኢያሱ፡ አሜን።

msItem.title: ʿAmda Ṣǝyon's donations
msItem.incipit:
በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ በ፭፻ወ፭፡ ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡
ወሀብኩ፡ አነ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ንጉሥ፡ ለአባ፡ መጣዕ፡ ዘአሀም፡
msItem.explicit: ከመ፡ ይትወሐስኒ፡ ፍቁርየ፡ መጣዕ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን።
msItem.note: [4.26-28] = CCR 19: (I ff. 6vb-7vb), according to AB, thus the same comments are applicabale to this part. I, however, decided to split what was considered one document.

msItem.title: Lālibalā's gʷǝlt to Ham
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ አጐልትኩ፡ አክሰምኩ፡ ወአመነይኩ፡ አነ፡ ሐፃኒ፡ ላሊበላ፡ ወስመ፡ መንግሥትየ፡ ገብረ፡ መስቀ ል፡ በ፬፻ወ፱። ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡
msItem.explicit: ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን፡ በቅድመ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ በአፉሁ፡ ለአቡነ፡ መጣዕ፡ በአፈ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ በኣፈ፡ ፫፻፲፡ ወ፰፡ ርቱዐነ፡ ሃይማኖት፡ በአፈ፡ ፲ወ፭ነቢያት፡ በአፈ፡ ፲ወ፪ሐዋርያት፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን።
msItem.incipit: ወእንዘ፡ ሀለው፡ ከል፡ ኤሆሙ፡ ኅቡረ፡ ላሊበላ፡ ንጉሥ፡ ወብእሲቱ፡ መስቀል፡ ክብራ፡ በአልተ፡ ቢሐት፡ ከመ፡ ይኩኖሙ፡ መድኃኒተ፡ ነፍሶሙ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡
msItem.explicit:
ዘንተ፡ ዘአጽሐፈ፡ ዐቃቤ፡ ሰዐተ፡ መጣዕ፡ ስመ፡ ልደቱ፡ ይርድአነ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ከመ፡ ምንኵስና
ሁ፡ ጸጋ፡ መጣዕ፡ ለዘአጽሐፈ፡ ስረዩ፡ ወባርኩነ፡ ኣሜን።

msItem.title: Return of lands by Krǝstos ʾAbuhu
msItem.incipit: :በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ። አግባእኩ፡ አነ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ አቡሁ፡ በአፈ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ንጉሥ፡ ተርካ፡ ዘቀዳሚ፡ ምድሮሙ፡ ለቅዱሳን፡

msItem.title: A note on women's ban at the monastery
msItem.incipit: አውሎግሶን፡ ጽደቁ፡ ዘአዘዙ፡ ንጉሥነ፡ ርቱዐ፡ ሃይማኖት፡ ሰይፈ፡ አርዓድ፡ ወስመ፡ መንግሥቱ፡ ቈስጠንጢኖስ፡
msItem.explicit:
ወእግዚአብሔር፡ ይዕቀብ፡ መንግሥቶ፡ ለንጉሥነ፡
ቆስጣንጢኖስ፡ ለዘጸውዐነ፡ እምኃጢአት፡ ከመ፡ ንትገሐስ።

msItem.title: Monks geneology
msItem.incipit: ልደቶሙ፡ ለአበው፡ በልብሰተ፡ አስኬማ፡ እንጦስ፡ ነሥአ፡ እመልአከ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ወወልደ፡ መቃርስሃ፡
msItem.explicit: ወያፍቅረነ፡ እግዚእ፡ ወለዶ፡ ለመሐሬ፡ እግዚእ፡ ወለዶ፡

smaller hand than . May be the same as .
brownish, red
Red ink was used to layout the letter. Each of three pages has the same mise en texte: three red lines from the top interchange with the black lines.
brownish

msItem.title: Letter to Carpianus and Eusebian Canons: 3 pages of letter + 7 pages of canons
msItem.incipit: እውሴብስ፡ ለቃጲያኖስ፡ ለዘ፡ ኣፈቅር፡ እኁየ፡ ፍሥሓ፡ ወዳኅና፡ ለከ፡ እምኃቤ፡ እግዚአ፡ ብሔር፡ አሞንስ፡ እንከ፡ አሌክሰንደርያዊ፡ በብዙኅ፡ አስተኃምሞ፡ ወጺሂቀ፡ ገብረ፡
msItem.explicit: ወጸሐፍኩ፡ ዓዲ፡ ውስተ፡ ወንጌል፡ ትእምርተ፡ አኀዘ፡ ዘበ፡ ሲራክ፡ ኀበ፡ ገብረ፡ ተአምረ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ወኃበሂ፡ አዘዘ፡ ወኃበሂ፡ ገሠጸ፡ ወኃበሂ፡ መሃረ፡ ወኃበሂ፡ ነበረ፡ ወትእምርተ፡ አኀዘ፡ ማዕተበ፡ መስቀል።

msItem.title: Epistle of Eusebius to Carpianus (General record)

layout.p: within the decoration with arches, one column or tables

handNote.t:date: the script can be considered contemporary to KingLālibālā
handNote.seg: black
Same as
handNote.note: Some strokes to mark numerals seem to have been added later.

handNote.seg: black
black

decoDesc.summary: According to the foliation introduced by Alessandro Bausi, II f.1v contains a big cross of one-page size. It is followed by 11 illustrated pages, II ff. 2r-7r. These pages include the Eusebian Apparatus (3+7 arrangement), followed by a «Fontana della vita» or «tempietto» (II f.7r) or θόλος, the Short Cycle, set under arches made of coloured bands. The manuscript’s decorative features, Blütenblattstil were not dated precisely, but the XIV century was proposed as a terminus non poste quem. They appear to all be by the same artist.

decoNote.desc: The first miniature according to the sequence attested by Carlo Conti Rossini, Roger Schneider and Alessandro Bausi contains a big cross of one-page size, belonging to the same style as the rest.
The Epistle of Eusebius to Carpianus (General record) runs between and is placed under coloured arches, ornamented variously, that rest on curtained columns.

The Canons of Eusebios (General record) run between . The artists adopts the same decorative devices used to frame the preceding letter. The Canons are placed under coloured arches, ornamented variously, that rest on curtained columns.

A representation of the Tempietto follows, as is conventional, the Eusebian Apparatus. Highly stylized, it features four columns, an interlace pattern, and a low chancel. Above its cross-topped roof, there are two ostrich birds, sitting on the both sides of a tree. The caption inside the Tempietto, which normally highlights its symbolism, is vanishing but still visible. ኑባሬ፡ ሥርዐት፡ ዘኅብሩ፡ አርባዕቱ፡ ወንጌላት።

item.q: ዘማርያም። እግዚእትየ፡ ማርያም፡ እስመ፡ በዝንቱ፡ ደመ፡ ወልድ፡ ተቀደሱ፡ ቅዱሳን፡ እስመ፡ በዝንቱ፡ ኅብስት፡ ወበዝንቱ፡ ጽዋዐ፡ በደኀሪት፡ ዕለት፡ ኅቱማኒሆሙ፡ ይትረከቡ፡ ሰአሊ፡ ለነ፡ ማርያም፡ በግርምት፡ ዕለት፡ ከመ፡ ይምሐረነ። dotted line ይቤ፡ እግዚእ፡ አክብሩ፡ ሰንበተ፡ ተገበሩ፡ ጽድቀ፡ ወፈድፋድስ፡ ለማርያም፡ አክብርዋ፡ እስመ፡ ኵሉ፡ ዘሰአለ፡ ኀቤሃ፡ ተዐስዮ፡ ፍሥሐ። dotted lineእለ፡ ጐዩ፡ እምነውር፡ እምዝ፡ ዓለም፡ ነኪር፡ እምወርቅ፡ ወእምብሩር፡ ዘይትበደር፡ አክሊለ፡ ነሥኡ፡ ዘሕብሩ፡ ሱሩረ፡ ከመ፡ እንስሳ፡ ገዳም፡ ውስተ፡ አርእስተ፡ አድባር፡ ነበሩ፡ ከመ፡ ኀረ//፡ በጻማ፡ ተገበሩ፡ እንዘ፡ ይጸንሑ፡ ብዕለ፡ ዘአልቦ፡ መምስለ

q.ab: በስመ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ። ተዝከሩ፡ ለበርናባስ፡ እም፡ ፳። ለሶንይ፡ ይምሐሮ፡ እግዚኣብሔር፡ በሰማያት። ወይደምሮ፡ ምስለ፡ ጻድቃን። ። ወለእለ፡ ይገብሩ፡ ተዝካሮ፡ እመሂ፡ አንስት፡ ወእመሂ፡ ተባዕት፡ ይምሐሮሙ፡ እግዚኣብሔር፡ በጸሎቱ፡ ወይስረይ፡ ኀጢአቶሙ፡ ለዓለም፡ አሜን።

desc.title: Gabra Masqal's donation of eight fields

q.seg: ወሀብኩ፡ አነ፡ ገብረ፡ መስቀል፡ በአሃም፡ ፷፡ ገራህት፡ ለቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ወከመ፡ ይግበሩ፡ ተዝካሮ፡ ለመጣዕ፡ ከመ፡ ይትወሀሰኒ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ እመቦ፡ ዘተኀየሎ፡ ወዘአገሎ፡ ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን። በአፈ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ።

msItem.title: ʿAmda Ṣǝyon's donations
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ በ፭፻ወ፭፡ ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡ ወሀብኩ፡ አነ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ንጉሥ፡ ለአባ፡ መጣዕ፡ ዘአሀም፡
msItem.explicit: ከመ፡ ይትወሐስኒ፡ ፍቁርየ፡ መጣዕ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን። ።
msItem.incipit: ወለንጉሥነ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ዘአዕበዮ፡ እምነገሥተ፡ ምድር፡
msItem.explicit: ምስለ፡ ዳዊት፡ ወቈስጠንጢኖስ፡ ወአኖርዮስ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን።
msItem.incipit: ወአንትሙሂ፡ ቅዱሳን፡ እለ፡ ሀለውክሙ፡ ውስተ፡ መካነ፡ መጣዕ፡ ጸልዩ፡ ለንጉሥ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡
msItem.explicit: ወበውስተ፡ ማኅበርሂ፡ ወኀበ፡ ተጋባእክሙሂ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን። ።

msItem.title: Gʷǝlt by Bǝlen Sābā
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ በ፭፻፡ ወ፯፡ ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡ ወሀብኩ፡ ኣነ፡ ብሌን፡ ሰባ፡ በዐልተ፡ ቢሐት፡ ብእሲቱ:ለዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ንጉሥ፡
msItem.explicit: የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ትዕግሥተ፡ ከመ፡ እዮብ፡ አሜን። የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ መዊአ፡ ከመ፡ ኢያሱ፡ አሜን።
msItem.incipit: ወለይእቲኒ፡ ብሌን፡ ሰባ፡ በዐልተ፡ ቢሐት፡ የሀባ፡ እግዚኣብሔር፡ ፍቅረ፡ ምስለ፡ ምታ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡
msItem.explicit: ወከመ፡ ኆጻ፡ ባሕር፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን። ወይበል፡ ኵሉ፡ ሕዝብ፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን።
msItem.incipit: ወኵልክሙ፡ እለ፡ አንበብክሙ፡ ጸልዩ፡
msItem.explicit: በሰማይ፡ ወበምድር፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን።

msItem.title: Grant to Māryām za-Sa‘agā by ‘Amda Ṣǝyon
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ አነ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ወሀብኩ፡ አንፈ፡ በዐት፡ ማርያም፡ ዘሰዐጋ፡ ሕፄን፡
msItem.explicit: ወበማርያም፡ ወላዲተ፡ አምላክ፡ ውጉዛነ፡ ለይኩኑ፡ አሜን።

msItem.title: Return of lands by Krǝstos ʾAbuhu
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ አግባእኩ፡ አነ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ አቡሁ፡ በአፈ፡ ዐምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ንጉሥ፡ ተርካ፡ ዘቀዳሚ፡ ምድሮሙ፡ ለቅዱሳን፡
msItem.explicit: ወእመቦ፡ ዘደምሰሳ፡ ወዘቀደዳ፡ በጽልሑት፡ ከማሁ፡ ለይኩን፡ ውጉዘ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን። ወአሜን።

handNote.seg: CCR dated all the texts in this range to the XVI century.
CCR dated this hand to the XVII century.

item.q: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ወሀብኩ፡ አነ፡ አምደ፡ ጽዮን፡ ለአባ፡ መጣዕ፡ ዘንተ፡ ጕልተ፡ ጽዳእ፡ አሲራ፡ ለባሲሮ፡ ገማእ፡ ዜና፡ ኩዶ፡ አድ፡ ሕልሞ፡ ኦና፡ ሳባይ፡ ስናጋ፡ ማርያሌ፡ ሠርዔ፡ ለጎ፡ ወንከብ፡ ገዳሞሙ፡ ለመነኮሳት፡ ቤተ፡ ሰማእት፡ አናንያ፡ አዛርያ፡ ወሚሳኤል፡ ከመ፡ ይትወሀሰኒ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ እመቦ፡ ዘተአገሎ፡ ወዘተኃየሎ፡ ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን።

item.q: ምድረ፡ ቴድረር፡ በሐምሐም፡ ቀላይ፡ ወባናዕሌ፡ ወበአረገን። በክኤቲ፡ ፈልግ፡ ወኀቦ፡ ጕልተ፡ ለቅዱስ፡ ሊባኖስ፡ ንጉሥነ፡ ገብረ፡ መስቀል፡ ከመ፡ ይኩኖ፡ መርሐ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት። ዘወሀቦ፡ ጕልተ፡ ኢይትኴቊ፡ እስመ፡ ብዙኀ፡ ውእቱ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡ ፡

msItem.title: Sulla devozione per le vesti di Libānos
msItem.incipit: ጸሐፍነ፡ ኀቢረነ፡ ወሠሚረነ፡ ተጋቢአነ፡ ቅዱሳን፡ ዐቃቤ፡ ሰዓት፡ ዘቤተ፡ መጣዕ፡
msItem.explicit: በቅድመ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን፡ ለይኩን፡ ለይኩን፡ አሜን።

handNote.seg: CCR dated this hand to the XVII century.

item.q: ዘአጽሐፍኩ፡ አነ፡ ሕዝብ፡ ሤመ፡ ዓቃቤ፡ ሰዓት፡ ዘቤተ፡ መጣዕ፡ ትስመር፡ ጽዮን፡ ገረደ፡ መጣዕ፡ ወተዳቦ፡ ብእሲሃ፡ በዘ፡ ፈረዩ፡ ወጸገዩ፡ ገንዞተ፡ መጣዕ፡ ዘእንበለ፡ ዘይትለአኩ፡ ለመካን። ለእመቦ፡ ዘተሐየሎሙ፡ ወተአገሎሙ፡ ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን፡ በአፈ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡

item.q: አፈ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ወበአፉሁ፡ ለአቡነ፡ መጣዕ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን።
item.note: [5.8.10]: (II f. 12rb e 12vb, bianco f. 12va) [simile al precedente, sulle fasce di Libānos, non reperibile in CCR], according to AB.

item.q: በ፴ወ፭፡ ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡ ወበመዋዕሊሁ፡ ለንጉሥነ፡ ዘርአ፡ ያዕቆብ፡ ወስመ፡ መንግሥቱ፡ ቈስጠንጢኖስ፡ ወእምአመ፡ ነግሠ፡ በ፳ወ፭፡ ዓመት፡ ዘወሀበ፡ ዓምደ፡ ለቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ዘመጣዕ፡ ተስፈ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ስዩም፡ ግሎመከደ፡ ወልዱ፡ ለይስሞ፡ ይባርክ፡ ነፍሱ፡ ወብእሲቱ፡ ወውሉዱ፡ ወይጽሐፍ፡ ስሞ፡ ውስተ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ሕይወት፡ በጸሎቱ፡ ለቅዱስ፡ መጣዕ፡ ወእንትሙሂ፡ ቅዱሳን፡ ዝክርዎ፡ ወኢትርስዕዎ፡ በጸሎትክሙ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን

msItem.title: Restoration of Tarkā by Tansǝ’a Krǝstos
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ዘወሀቡ፡ ቀደምት፡ ተርከ፡ ለአቡነ፡ ሊባኖስ፡ ወአነሂ፡ ተንሥአ፡ ክርስቶስ፡
msItem.explicit: ዘኢይትፈታሕ፡ በጾም፡ ወበጸሎት፡ በአፉሁ፡ ለአቡነ፡ መጣዕ፡ አሜን።

msItem.title: Grant by Yǝśmǝka ʾƎgzi’
msItem.incipit: ወሀብኩ፡ አነ፡ ይሥምከ፡ እግዚእ፡ ሥዩመ፡ ኀማሳን፡ ለአባ፡ መጣዕ፡ ዘአሀም፡ ፈዋሴ፡ ዱያን፡
msItem.explicit: ቃለ፡ መክብብ፡ ዘይቤ፡ ኵሉ፡ ከንቱ፡ ከንቱ፡ ውእቱ፡ ዘኢገብረ፡ ሠናየ፡ በሕይወቱ። ወበዐጋማሂ፡ አርታዕቱዕኩ፡ ጕልተ።

handNote.seg: CCR dated this hand to the XVII-XVIII century.
CCR dated this hand to the XVI century.

msItem.title: Lālibalā's gʷǝlt to Ham
msItem.incipit: ወእንዘ፡ ሀለዉ፡ ክልኤሆሙ፡ ኅቡረ፡ ላሊበላ፡ ንጉሥ፡ ወብእሲቱ፡ መስቀል፡ ክብራ፡ በአልተ፡ ቢሐት፡ ከመ፡ ይኩኖሙ፡ መድኀኒተ፡ ነፍሶሙ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡
msItem.explicit: ዘንተ፡ ዘአጽሐፈ፡ ዐቃቤ፡ ስዐተ፡ መጣዕ፡ ስመ፡ ልደቱ፡ ይርድአነ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ከመ፡ ምንኵስናሁ፡ ጸጋ፡ መጣዕ፡ ለዘአጽሐፈ፡ ወለዘጸሐፈ፡ ስረዩ፡ ወባርኩነ፡ ኣሜን።

msItem.title: Land grant by Yǝrdǝʾanna Krǝstos, bəgwənāy of Ṣəlālā, ‘aqābe sa‘āt of Maṭā‘
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ወሀብኩ፡ አነ፡ ይርድአነ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ብጕናይ፡ ዘጽላላ፡ ዐቃቤ፡ ሰዐተ፡ መጣዕ፡
msItem.explicit: ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን፡ በዝ፡ ዓለም፡ ወበዘ፡ ይመጽእ፡ ዓለም፡ ውጉዘ፡ ለይኩን፡ በአፉሁ፡ ለአቡነ፡ መጣዕ፡ ወበአፈ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ አሜን። ይኩን፡ ለይኩን። ። ።

msItem.incipit: >ሣህል፡ ወረደ፡ ወሀብኩ፡ ለመጣዕ፡ ሰርፍ፡
msItem.note: [5.14]: (f. 14v) [non reperibile in CCR], according to AB.

handNote.seg: CCR dated this hand to the XVII century. This is the same hand as
CCR dated this hand to the XVII century.

decoNote.desc: A chain is present at the top of .

desc.title: Gabra Masqal's grant of Sar’e etc

item.q: አባ፡ መጣዕ፡ ዘወሀብክዎ፡ አነ፡ ገብረ፡ መስቀል፡ አእሚርየ፡ ከመ፡ የኃልፍ፡ ንብረተ፡ ዝንቱ፡ ዓለም
ʾAbbā Maṭāʿ what I, Gabra Masqal, gave, knowing that the possession of the world will pass.
item.note: [5.12] = cf. CCR 1: (II f. 14r margine superiore), according to AB.

msItem.title: Land grant by Takasta Bǝrhan, nǝbura ʾǝd and ‘aqabe sa‘āt za-beta Maṭā‘
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ አነ፡ ተከሥተ፡ ብ፡ ነብር፡ እደ፡ ሰዐት፡ ዘመጣዕ፡ ኩ፡ ለመጣዕ፡ ባአ፡ ፫፡ ገራህተ፡
msItem.explicit: በ፩፡ ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን።
msItem.note:
[5.14] = CCR 11: (II f. 15r), according to AB. Carlo Conti Rossini comments on p.195: Scrittura molto arcaica, non posteriore al secolo XIV o XV. Il folio e lacerato in piu punti superiormente, e nell’ultimo tratto l’inchiostro e in parte svanito, di guisa che l’atto in parte e illegibile L’evangelo d’oro di Dabra LibānosRendiconti della Reale Accademia dei Lincei, Classe di scienze morali, storiche e filologicheCarloConti Rossini1901Serie quinta, 10177-219http://periodici.librari.beniculturali.it/visualizzatore.aspx?anno=1901&ID_testata=30&ID_periodico=11855195
.

msItem.title: Restoration of gʷǝlt by ʾƎngǝdā ʾƎgzi’
msItem.note: [5.16] = CCR 10: (II f. 15v), according to AB. Carlo Conti Rossini comments on p. 194 : Scrittura del secolo XVI: sul v del folgio su cui e steso l’atto 55. Croce ansata e fregio in margine L’evangelo d’oro di Dabra LibānosRendiconti della Reale Accademia dei Lincei, Classe di scienze morali, storiche e filologicheCarloConti Rossini1901Serie quinta, 10177-219http://periodici.librari.beniculturali.it/visualizzatore.aspx?anno=1901&ID_testata=30&ID_periodico=11855194.

handNote.seg: CCR dated this hand to the XIV-XV century.
CCR dated this hand to the XVIcentury.

item.q: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ጸሐፍነ፡ ተዝካረ፡ ዕረፍቶሙ፡ ለቅዱሳን፡ አኃዊነ። ፍጹመ፡ አምላክነ፡ አመ፡ ፳ወ፬፡ ለመስከረም፡ ወአመ፡ ፲ወ፫፡ ለጥቅምት፡ ይም እግዚእ፡ ወአመ፡ ፳ወ፪፡ ለታኅሣሥ፡ መኒት፡ ማርያም፡ ወዘይገብር፡ ተዝካሮሙ፡
item.note: [5.14]: (f. 15r) [non reperibile in CCR], accoding to AB.

msItem.title: Restoration of gʷǝlt by ‘Amda Ṣǝyon/Garba Masqal
msItem.incipit:
በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ አጽሐፍኩ፡ አነ፡
ገብረ፡ መስቀል፡ ንጉሥ። በ፭፻፲ወ፪። ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡
msItem.explicit: የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ጥበበ፡ ከመ፡ ሰሎሞን፡ አሜን። የሀቦ፡ እግዚኣብ
msItem.incipit:
ወለንጉሥነ፡
ገብረ፡ መስቀል፡ የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ መንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ ህየንተ፡ ሕዳጥ፡ ብዙኅ፡ አሜን።
msItem.explicit: ወያግርር፡ ጸሮ፡ ወጻላእቶ፡ አሜን። ወይስረይ፡ ኃጢአቶ፡ ወይደምስስ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ዕዳሁ፡ አሜን። የሀቦ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ጥበበ፡ ከመ፡ ሰሎሞን፡ አሜን። የሀቦ፡ እግዚኣብ

handNote.seg: CCR dated this hand to the XV-XVI century.

msItem.title: Gʷǝlt by Yā‘bika ʾƎgzi’
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ በ፭፻፫፡ ዓመተ፡ ምሕረት፡ ወሀበ፡ ያዕቢከ፡ እግዚእ፡ በዘ፡ ይገብሩ፡ ተዝካሮ፡ ለመጣዕ፡ እለ፡ የኀድሩ፡ ውስተ፡ መካን፡ መነኮሳት፡ ጕልት፡
msItem.explicit: ከመ፡ ወርኅ፡ ወብሩሀ፡ ከመ፡ ፀሓይ፡ ወበዕለተ፡ ሞቱ፡ ይኈልቆ፡ ምስለ፡ ጻድቃን፡ ወሰማዕት፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን፡ ወአሜን። ።

msItem.title: Land grant by ‘Ǝqbā ʾƎgzi’
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ወሀብነ፡ ዘንተ፡ ገራውሀ፡ በአሀም፡ አነ፡ ዕቅባ፡ እግዚእ፡ ወእግዚእ፡ ይባ፡ መሬት፡ መንደድ፡
msItem.explicit: ውጉዛነ፡ ለይኵኑ፡ በአፉሁ፡ ለእግዚእነ፡ ወበአፉሁ፡ ለመጣዕ፡ ወበአፈ፡ ፫፻፡ ምእት፡ ፲ወ፰፡ ርቱዓነ፡ ሃይማኖት፡ ዘኢይትፈታሕ፡ በጾም፡ ወበጸሎት፡ ውጉዛነ፡ ለይኵኑ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን። ወአሜን።

handNote.seg: CCR dated this hand to the XVI century.
CCR dated this hand to the XVI century.
CCR dated this hand to the XVI century.

item.q: ዘወሀብኩሰ፡ ንዋይየ፡ ከመ፡ ይትወሐሰኒ፡ መጣዕ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን። ወብዙትሂ፡ ወ፩፡ ላህም፡ ወሀብኩ፡ ለቅዱስ፡ መጣዕ፡ ከመ፡ ይትወሐሰኒ፡ በመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት፡ ወከመ፡ ይግባሩ፡ ተዝካርየ፡ ደቂቀ፡ መካን፡ ምስለ፡ እኅትየ፡ እግዚእ፡ ባ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን።
item.note: This text is not to be found in Conti Rossini's edition. This made Roger Schneider to think that this text was added after Conti Rossini did his work.

msItem.title: Gospel donation by Takasta Bǝrhan, nǝbura ʾǝd and ‘aqabe sa‘āt za-beta Maṭā‘
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ተከሥተ፡ ብርሃን፡ ንቡረ፡ እድ፡ ዘአክሱም፡ ወንጌል፡ ወኅብረተ፡ ቃላት፡ ወአርአያ፡ ኢየሱስ፡
msItem.explicit: ወዕለተ፡ ተዝካሩ፡ አመ፡ ፲፡ ለተሕሳስ፡ ለዓለም።

msItem.title: Note by ‘aqābē sa‘āt ʾAsfǝḥā
msItem.incipit: ወአነሂ፡ ዐቃቤ፡ ሰዓት፡ አስፍሐ፡ ወደቂቀ፡ መካነሂ፡ ኀቢረነ፡ ወሠሚረነ፡ ሠራዕነ፡
msItem.explicit: በንዋየ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ እምይእዜ፡ እስከ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን።

msItem.title: Restoration of gʷǝlt by Tasfāna ʾƎgzi’
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ዘአግባእኩ፡ ጕልተ፡ ዘተገፍዐ፡ አነ፡ ተስፋነ፡ እግዚእ፡ ለመጣዕ፡ ዘአሀም፡
msItem.explicit: ወተርጓሚሁ፡ አነ፡ አስፍሐ፡ ዐቃቤ፡ ሰዐት፡ ዘመጣዕ፡

handNote.seg: CCR dated this hand to the XVI century.

item.q: በአኰቴተ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ወሀብኩ፡ አነ፡ ማርያም፡ ኀይላ፡ ለመጣዕ፡ ገራህት፡ ወይትወሐሰኒ፡ መጣዕ፡ ለመንግሥተ፡ ሰማያት። ወከመ፡ ይትወሐሶ፡ ለአቡየ፡ ዘውሕሱ። ወከመ፡ ተዝካርነ፡ መጣዕ፡ ለዓለመ፡ ዓለም፡ አሜን።

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: The small quire at the beginning of the text block, bound together, is a material unit due to the size of the leaves in comparison to the rest of the manuscript.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: The small quire at the beginning bound together is a quire unit, being bound together.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: The eight leaves within the small quire are of a different parchment and are thus a separate material unit. There is no sign that these four bifolia where once separated and thus there is no sign that this was a quire or a production unit separate from the rest of the small quire. It is only part of it, or rather the two outer bifolia where, in the same act of production, used to surround the inner leaves, which share in layout, ruling and writing.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: The small quire constitutes one production unit being bound into a single quire.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: All other leaves are of the same size, the one of the Gospel and constitute thus a unit

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: the small quire was added to the Manuscript.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This a transformation A1 Addition of material support and content

relation.name: sdc:produces
relation.desc: There are therefore a UniCirc before the transformation and one after.

relation.name: sdc:resultsIn
relation.desc: The Manuscript as we know it in terms of material correspond to this circulation unit.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: The Manuscript before that addition is another circulation unit we can be sure about.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: For the latest additions, which were presumably not seen by Carlo Conti Rossini, we can assume they where made to the circulation unit which already had the small quire. This is however hypothetical, as it may also be for other reasons that Conti Rossini did not list these texts.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This a transformation A2 Addition of material support and content.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: Each of these additions is a production unit, involved into the one group transformation which groups different acts which we cannot further distinguish at the moment.

relation.name: sdc:resultsIn
relation.desc: The unit before and after the transformation are UniCirc, but because we cannot order them one by one we can only group additions into one transformation. The issue is determining to which UniCirc the additions where made and this also can be done only for some additions.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: What we have now having been subject to additions as a whole is a unit which has been subject to a transformation UA1, that is, a union followed by additions.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: The leaves of the same size of the Gospel after the small quire have detached from one another and we cannot say how they fit together. This occurred probably before the small quire was added, if this was the result of a copy from the contents of some of these leaves, so the subject of the transformation is the circulation unit before the transformation which added the small quire.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This transformation is a simple division (D3).

relation.name: sdc:produces
relation.desc: This detachment however did not produce different units of circulation, as the leaves continued to circulate together.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: The two leaves with the end of the Gadla Qirqos, distinguished from the rest because of the ruling pattern and material, were probably taken from another, unknown, manuscript to produce the small quire at a point which we cannot say. We need to define this as a production unit separate from the one of the small quire which is now inside it.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: Also the small quire, whose content stability allows to think of a single production intention is also a production unit, however independent.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: The unit from the other manuscript was then subject to a transformation.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This transformation is a simple division (D2).

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: And was followed by the addition to a another unit.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: We thus have to say that also the unit with the other ruling pattern now inside this one was part of the same transformation

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This transformation is a an addition of support and contents (A1).

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: Also in this case we can group the transformation into a single complex one. This is probably a transformation MA1, where a codex has been somehow deprived of two bifolia to make our quire which contains the homogeneous one. The additions on folios 11 and 12 use the ruling in some way, but this does not tell us if these where there already or not. If we had organized a table by quire, which was not suitable for this example, it would become clear that a convergent discontinuity was present here. In our table this turns out into two convergent discontinuities.

relation.name: sdc:produces
relation.desc: The result of this is thus the observable production unit.

relation.name: sdc:resultsIn
relation.desc: The transformation also consequently results in a new circulation unit.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: If the texts (UniCont) on p1 f.11v and 12v where already there when the leaves where used to produce uniprod1, we do not know. CCR dates them later than the text of the Gadla Qirqos, which however does not help in this respect. They where added to that material in any case.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This a transformation A2, an addition of content.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: The following are permutations of type P1, changes in the order of the folios, which produce a new UniCirc, but not a new UniProd. After cross checking convergent discontinuities in the table view, we can safely say that the folio numbered by Schneider 17/18, the same as Bausi II 15, is the leaf which CCR had before the one with the cross in his description, the ancient leaf. If we follow Conti Rossini description, the two leaves after the small quire are not there. In facts photo 001 by Schneider shows that the sequence sees the end of the small quire and then the folio which has the coptic cross on the verso. Of all folios available the one which carries the texts which Conti Rossini dates the earliest is the one which we now numbered 27, which is delimited by convergent discontinuities, and was referenced as said above previously. We can thus say that there was at least one permutation of the order of the leaves, before they were photographed in 1975 and that this one moved to its current position.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This is a permutation of folios. For the msPart containing the Eusebian letter and the canons, identified by decoration and content unity, the sequence at CCR time was, with reference to the current pagination 27 (the leaf with old texts), 13 (cross on verso), 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19 (tempio on recto).

relation.name: sdc:resultsIn
relation.desc: The permutation results in a unit of circulation only.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc:
The continuity between 19 and 26 may be based on the convergence of discontinuities of
content (CCR7)
material
decoration
layout (1 col)
eventually the two lines text on top of 19r and 26r.
We can thus say that another permutation occurred with respect to the latest photos. This is easily confirmed and dated after 1975, since in the photos of Schneider the current folio 26 does follow directly on 19 and is paginated with 3 for the recto and 4 for the verso.

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This is a another permutation of folios.

relation.name: sdc:resultsIn
relation.desc: The further permutation results in a further unit of circulation only.

relation.name: sdc:undergoesTransformation
relation.desc: There is another observable permutation which affects the group of leaves of the same size of the once containing the Gospel. The leaves which Schneider paginated as 7/8 and 9/10 moved by the time Bausi took his photographs of these folia in 1993 to their current placement at 24 and 25 (Bausi II 12 and II 13). It may have appeared obvious to someone that in their position at that time they interrupted the continuity of a content Unit (CCR 20).

relation.name: sdc:hasTransformationModel
relation.desc: This is a another permutation of folios.

relation.name: sdc:resultsIn
relation.desc: The further permutation results in a further unit of circulation only.

relation.name: skos:exactMatch
relation.desc: This unit, which contains what was last seen corresponds to the described object.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: We can then define a reconstructed production unit which represents the state of the leaves sorrounding the decorated leaves and is possibly closer to the state observed by Conti Rossini. To this unit other texts which are discontinuous with the rest and are not those added later may have been added, e.g. CCR 8.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: A unit, corresponding roughly to a fascicule circulated until Schneider took photos as 20, 24, 25, 21, 22, 23. It is impossible to say more on this unit, whose unity at some point may be attested by a univocal deletion intervention.

relation.name: sdc:constituteUnit
relation.desc: However the layout of 24 and 25 stands out as a separate unit, characterized by the two columns layout and the blank first column on the verso of both leaves, and thus to define a further unit of the remaining leaves sharing the one column layout and the dotted line separators.
